name: key-sorting-surrogate
description: JCS key sorting for non-BMP characters (surrogate pairs) per RFC 8785

tests.name: emoji_vs_bmp
tests.description: Emoji U+1F600 uses surrogate pair (D83D, DE00). High surrogate D83D (55357) < FFFF (65535) in UTF-16 comparison

tests.name: two_emoji
tests.description: Same high surrogate, different low surrogate: DE00 < DE01

tests.name: emoji_in_key_name
tests.description: Keys with emoji suffix: z (122) < D83D (55357), so keyz sorts first, then emoji keys in surrogate order

tests.name: mixed_bmp_and_supplementary
tests.description: U+10000 (first supplementary char) has high surrogate D800 (55296) < FFFF (65535)

tests.name: musical_symbols
tests.description: Musical symbols U+1D100 and U+1D11E - same high surrogate D834

implementation_notes.utf16_conversion: For code points > U+FFFF: high = 0xD800 + ((cp - 0x10000) >> 10), low = 0xDC00 + ((cp - 0x10000) & 0x3FF)
implementation_notes.comparison: Compare as sequence of uint16 values, not as Unicode code points
implementation_notes.go_note: Go strings are UTF-8; must convert to UTF-16 code units for correct JCS sorting

notes: RFC 8785 Section 3.2.3: For characters outside BMP (U+10000+), comparison is by UTF-16 code units (surrogate pairs), not by code point value. High surrogate values (0xD800-0xDBFF = 55296-56319) are numerically LESS than U+FFFF (65535).
